A Catoblepas is a large quadrupedal mammal built low to the ground on four thick legs ending in wide cloven hooves that distribute its weight across soft mud without sinking. The body is a barrel of dense muscle covered in loose, wrinkled hide that hangs in folds around the shoulders and flanks, dark grey-brown to near black depending on variant. Coarse bristly hairs sprout in uneven patches along the spine and between the shoulders. The creature stands approximately 4 ft at the shoulder at level 1 and is nearly 6 ft across at its widest point, giving it a squat rectangular profile wider than it is tall.

The head is disproportionately large, accounting for nearly a third of the creature's total body length. It is blunt and heavy, consisting primarily of thick bone and dense cartilage, and hangs so low that the snout clears the ground by only a few inches when the creature walks. The neck is heavily muscled where it joins the skull, thickening into a distinct hump behind the head, but remains permanently bent downward under the weight. The face is broad and flat with a short pig-like snout, small watery eyes recessed under a prominent brow ridge, and a wide mouth that angles downward at the corners. Two short tusks curve upward from the lower jaw, yellowed and worn from grinding against roots and stones. The ears are small, rounded, and lie flat against the head. A Catoblepas at rest appears sluggish to the point of torpor. Its head hangs motionless. Its eyes are half-closed. Its mouth hangs slightly open. This is not sleep. It is the creature's permanent resting state.

The Catoblepas is slow moving and deliberate in all its actions. It spends the majority of its life standing in shallow stagnant water, feeding on marsh grass, algae, and aquatic roots. It is not predatory and does not hunt. It is, however, highly territorial and will respond to intrusion with immediate hostility. When approached, the Catoblepas lifts its head a few inches, fixes its gaze on the intruder, and exhales a dense cloud of toxic vapor. It makes no distinction between a threat and a bystander. Anything that enters its territory is treated the same. The toxic breath is produced by a pair of specialized glands located in the throat directly behind the jaw. The glands continuously generate a concentrated aerosolized toxin that the Catoblepas stores in its sinus cavities and releases at will. The vapor is heavier than air and rolls across the ground in a low bank, pale yellow-green and reeking of sulfur. Vegetation struck by the vapor withers to black slime within seconds. Small animals die almost instantly, their flesh graying and separating from the bone. On larger creatures the toxin calcifies living tissue from the inside outward. Muscle and skin harden into a brittle grey-white stone that cracks under its own weight. A person exposed to a concentrated dose will petrify in place, frozen in whatever position they held when the cloud reached them.

Catoblepas reproduction is infrequent and poorly documented. Males and females tolerate each other's presence for a brief period once per year, during which they remain in proximity without apparent interaction. The female gestates a single calf for approximately six months and gives birth in shallow water. The calf is born fully furred and mobile. Its toxin glands begin functioning around three months of age, at which point the mother drives it away. A Catoblepas reaches level 1 after its first year and gains 1 level per year thereafter. They live approximately 30 years in the wild.

When killed, a Catoblepas offers limited harvestable material. The hide is thin, loose, and permeated with trace toxins. Anyone wearing armor or clothing made from untreated Catoblepas Hide must make a CON save (DC 10) at the start of each hour of wear or their limbs go numb, imposing Disadvantage on STR and DEX checks and gaining the Nauseated condition until the clothing is removed and 10 minutes have passed. Proper tanning removes this effect but the resulting leather is of poor quality regardless. The meat is pale and greasy with a texture similar to pork and a gamy, swampy flavor, accompanied by a mild numbing sensation on the tongue from residual toxins. Eating the meat requires a CON save (DC 10) or the consumer gains the Poisoned condition for 1 hour. Cooking reduces the DC to 6 but does not fully neutralize the toxin.

The only part of value is the toxin gland. Located in the throat behind the jaw, the gland is a sac-like organ that stores concentrated toxin in liquid form before it is aerosolized. If extracted within an hour of death, the gland can be preserved and used as a weapon. Squeezing the gland releases a pressurized spray in a 10 ft cone. Creatures in the cone must make a CON save (DC 10 + 1 per 2 levels of the source Catoblepas) or become Petrified for 1 turn and take 1d6 + half the source Catoblepas's Affinity (rounded down) in Toxin damage (or Corruption damage for a Corrupted gland). On a success, they take half damage and are not Petrified. After each use, roll 1d20 and add half your Toxin, Poison, or Corruption affinity (whichever is highest, rounded down). On a 15 or higher, the gland retains its charge and can be used again. On a 14 or below, the gland runs dry and must be refilled. Every 3 successful uses increases the DC of both the attack and the usage roll by 1. To refill an empty gland, spend 10 minutes channeling mana into it and roll 1d20 plus half your Toxin, Poison, or Corruption affinity (whichever is highest): on an 18 or higher, it regains one charge. The gland can only hold one charge at a time regardless of its source.
###### Variant Descriptions

**Catoblepas (DR 4):** The standard variant found in shallow swamps, bogs, bayous, and the edges of stagnant lakes. Stands approximately 4 ft at the shoulder and weighs around 1,200 lbs at level 1. The hide is a mottled dark grey-brown. The head hangs roughly a foot off the ground at rest. The tusks are short and used for uprooting marsh grass. Catoblepas are solitary and territorial, claiming a stretch of riverbank or a single pond as their range, and will tolerate other creatures at distances beyond 20 ft. Closer intrusion is met with Toxic Breath. Locals in swamp regions identify Catoblepas territory by the dead patches of vegetation and greyed-out tree stumps that mark the boundary and avoid those areas.

**Corrupted Catoblepas (DR 8):** A variant twisted by prolonged exposure to necrotic or infernal energy, found in blighted moors, cursed swamps, and the fetid marshes around ancient battlefields. The hide is mottled grey-green with patches of black skin that weep a thin yellow fluid. The head is larger, the tusks longer and sharper, and the creature is noticeably heavier, standing 5 ft at the shoulder and weighing over 1,600 lbs at level 1. A constant low mist of pale yellow vapor drifts from the nostrils and mouth even at rest. The eyes are milky white and nonfunctional. Corrupted Catoblepas are actively hostile and will charge anything within their territory, which can span miles. Their Toxic Breath deals Corruption damage instead of Toxin and is a 25 ft cone. Other swamp predators avoid Corrupted Catoblepas territories entirely.
###### Unique Abilities
All Catoblepas share the following unique abilities regardless of variant.

- **Toxic Breath (Active, 3 Mana):** The Catoblepas exhales a cone of dense vapor. All creatures in the cone must make a CON save (DC 10 + Affinity). On a failure, they take 2d6 + Affinity Toxin damage (or Corruption damage for a Corrupted Catoblepas) and begin to petrify: their speed is halved and they have Disadvantage on STR and DEX checks for 2 turns. If a creature fails this save twice within the same combat, they become Petrified for 1 hour. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and does not begin to petrify. The vapor cloud lingers in the affected area for 2 rounds; any creature that enters or starts its turn in the cloud on a subsequent round must repeat the save or take 1d6 damage of the same type (no petrification on subsequent exposure). Plants and small vegetation in the cone die instantly. The cone size is 20 ft for a standard Catoblepas and 25 ft for a Corrupted Catoblepas.
- **Dead Zone (Passive):** The area within 10 ft of the Catoblepas is saturated with low-level toxin. Nonmagical vegetation withers and dies within 24 hours of the Catoblepas settling in an area. Creatures that start their turn within 10 ft of the Catoblepas must make a CON save (DC 8 + Affinity) or take 1d4 Toxin damage (or Corruption damage for a Corrupted Catoblepas). This damage does not trigger petrification. The Catoblepas is immune to its own Dead Zone and to the Dead Zone of other Catoblepas.
- **Cantankerous (Passive):** The Catoblepas cannot be Charmed, Frightened, or put to sleep by any magical or nonmagical effect. Any creature that attempts to mount, ride, or physically control the Catoblepas takes 1d6 Toxin damage (or Corruption damage) and is automatically pushed 5 ft away.
- **Rooting Charge (Active, 2 Mana):** The Catoblepas lowers its head and charges up to its speed in a straight line. All creatures in the line must make a DEX save (DC 10 + STR mod) or take 1d10 + STR mod bludgeoning damage and be knocked Prone. If the Catoblepas hits at least one creature, it may make a free Slam attack against the first creature it struck. The Catoblepas does not provoke opportunity attacks during this charge.

Variant-specific abilities are listed below.

- **Swamp Camouflage (Passive):** Standard Catoblepas only. While standing still in swamp or marsh terrain, the Catoblepas is nearly indistinguishable from a moss-covered boulder or a rotting log. Creatures attempting to spot it from more than 30 ft away must make a Spot check (DC 15) to recognize it as a living creature. This effect ends as soon as the Catoblepas moves or uses Toxic Breath.
- **Blight Cloud (Active, 4 Mana):** Corrupted Catoblepas only. The Corrupted Catoblepas releases a massive burst of corrupted vapor in a 30 ft radius centered on itself. This functions as Toxic Breath affecting all creatures in the area (dealing Corruption damage). The cloud is thick and opaque, blocking line of sight through the affected area for 3 rounds. Creatures inside the cloud have Disadvantage on all attacks and Spot checks. Usable once per combat.

**Standard Passives:** Thorns (toxic bristles along the spine), Scratch Resistance, Hardened Body (Corrupted only)
**Standard Actives:** Slam, Spit, Slow Field
### Statblock/Growth Table/Harvestable Materials for Catoblepas
> For more information on how to level up a monster see [[7.1 Monsters]].

The stat blocks below represent a level 1 Catoblepas of each variant. Higher level Catoblepas gain Vitality, Health, Mana, and stats as described in the leveling rules. Catoblepas gain 1 level per year of life. The variant is determined by the environment; a calf born in corrupted wetlands develops into a Corrupted Catoblepas regardless of parentage.

| Name                 | DR  | AC  | Speed | Type   | Affinity        | Gen Dmg Mod | Resistance            | Weakness             | Vitality   | Health    | Mana      | Stats (STR/CON/DEX/WIS/INT/CHA) | Size  |
| -------------------- | --- | --- | ----- | ------ | --------------- | ----------- | --------------------- | -------------------- | ---------- | --------- | --------- | ------------------------------- | ----- |
| Catoblepas           | 4   | 18  | 20 ft | Tanker | Toxin (+3)      | +1          | Toxin                 | Radiant, Solar       | 34d6 (119) | 14d6 (49) | 16d6 (56) | 26/28/8/10/4/4                  | Large |
| Corrupted Catoblepas | 8   | 20  | 20 ft | Tanker | Corruption (+5) | +3          | Corruption, Necrotic  | Infernal, Radiant    | 48d6 (168) | 22d6 (77) | 22d6 (77) | 30/32/8/10/4/4                  | Large |

| Name                 | Size  | Base Wt   | Wt/Lvl   | Base Lgth | Lgth/Lvl |
| -------------------- | ----- | --------- | -------- | --------- | -------- |
| Catoblepas           | Large | 1,200 lbs | +110 lbs | 7 ft      | +0.3 ft  |
| Corrupted Catoblepas | Large | 1,600 lbs | +140 lbs | 8 ft      | +0.4 ft  |

| Name                 | Hide                                 | Meat                                 | Unique Materials                        |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------- |
| Catoblepas           | Catoblepas Hide (12% of body weight) | Catoblepas Meat (25% of body weight) | Catoblepas Toxin Gland (1, approx 5 lbs) |
| Corrupted Catoblepas | Catoblepas Hide (12% of body weight) | Catoblepas Meat (25% of body weight) | Catoblepas Toxin Gland (1, approx 7 lbs) |

> Catoblepas Hide is poor quality leather that requires tanning to be safe to wear; untreated hide causes numbness and Nausea. Catoblepas Meat has a gamey, swampy flavor and is mildly toxic, requiring a CON save to eat safely. The Toxin Gland is a reusable short-range petrifying weapon powered by affinity. See [[2.6 Materials]] for full stats and pricing.
